message:
fix(vdbe): skip take_reg when MakeRecord sideband is active, add TRACK_R coverage

## Production fix

The INSERT hot path previously called `self.take_reg(record_reg)`
unconditionally before checking whether a MakeRecord sideband was
active. `take_reg` moves the value out of the register and replaces
it with `SqliteValue::Null`, which normally is a performance win
because it avoids an Arc/Blob clone on the record body. But it also
*materializes* the register value: for the sideband-optimized path
(where the record body lives in `self.make_record_buf` and
`self.make_record_sideband_reg == record_reg`), the `take_reg` call
would force the engine to wrap `make_record_buf` in a fresh
`Arc<[u8]>`-backed `SqliteValue::Blob` and store it in the register
just so the next line could move it out again. That is exactly the
cost the sideband path is designed to avoid.

Reorder the two to:

1. First check `sideband_active = self.make_record_sideband_reg ==
   record_reg && !self.make_record_buf.is_empty()`.
2. When the sideband is active, clear only the register subtypes
   (if any) and drop the register down to `SqliteValue::Null`
   directly without touching `take_reg`. The record bytes remain in
   `make_record_buf` and are taken into `sideband_buf` below via the
   existing `std::mem::take(&mut self.make_record_buf)` path.
3. When the sideband is *not* active, run `take_reg(record_reg)` as
   before to get the already-materialized blob/record register value.

Additionally, the MemDatabase fallback path that walks `self.db`
previously only knew how to decode records out of `record_val` via
`decode_record_with_metrics`. That is still correct for the
non-sideband path, but when the sideband is active, `record_val` is
`SqliteValue::Null` and the real bytes live in `sideband_buf`. The
MemDatabase branch now inspects `sideband_active` and parses
`sideband_buf` directly via `parse_record`, updating the
`FSQLITE_VDBE_RECORD_DECODE_CALLS_TOTAL` metric and per-value
decoded metrics identically to the non-sideband path. Result is
identical behavior across both insert backends with the sideband
optimization preserved in the MemDatabase case.

## TRACK_R (bd-) regression tests

Over 900 lines of new regression coverage for the MakeRecord
scratch-buffer reuse contract and the two-pass MakeRecord encoding
live in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`, tracked under the `track_r`
beads series. Highlights:

- `test_track_r_make_record_scratch_reuse_1000_records_no_realloc_after_first`
  — serialize 1000 records into the MakeRecord scratch buffer and
  verify that after the first allocation the scratch capacity never
  grows (i.e. every subsequent `serialize_record_iter_into` reuses
  the same buffer). Also emits a `track_r` eprintln with
  `alloc_count`, `scratch_capacity_bytes`, and `encode_time_ns` so
  the contract is observable in CI logs.
- `test_track_r_two_pass_matches_three_pass_proptest_10k_random` —
  proptest harness with a 10K case budget that generates random
  records (all SqliteValue variants, NaN floats, SmallText /
  heap-text mix, empty / boundary-sized blobs) and verifies that
  the fast two-pass MakeRecord encoder produces byte-identical
  output to the legacy three-pass reference encoder.
- `test_track_r_make_record_all_sqlite_types` — explicit coverage
  for every SqliteValue variant including integer boundaries (i8 /
  i16 / i24 / i32 / i48 / i64), ±Inf / NaN / zero floats, empty
  and max-len texts, and empty / small / large blobs.
- `test_track_r_make_record_empty_record` — zero-column edge case.
- `test_track_r_make_record_max_columns` — `MAX_COLUMN`-wide record
  verifying header and body sizes remain bounded.
- `test_track_r_make_record_insert_10k_oracle` — 10K row insert
  into a two-column table run against both fsqlite and rusqlite,
  verifying row-by-row parity on the readback. Uses the new
  `rusqlite` dev-dep on fsqlite-vdbe.
- `test_track_r_make_record_roundtrip_10k_records` — 10K synthesize
  / serialize / parse_record / assert parity loop.

A second cluster of tests targets the register value subsystem:

- `test_register_value_i64_store_and_retrieve`
- `test_register_text_cache_stays_valid_while_storage_cursor_row_is_pinned`
- `test_register_value_make_record_sideband_materializes_into_sqlite_blob_value`
  — verifies that when a sideband materialization *does* happen
  (e.g. because the record is consumed by code that requires an
  owned SqliteValue::Blob), the resulting blob matches the
  sideband bytes.
- `test_register_value_insert_avoids_make_record_blob_materialization`
  — positive assertion for the optimization this commit preserves:
  inserts that go through the sideband path must not increment
  `FSQLITE_VDBE_MAKE_RECORD_SIDEBAND_MATERIALIZATIONS_TOTAL`.
- `test_register_value_cursor_move_invalidates_cached_text`

Several new helper utilities back the tests:
`track_r_log_metrics`, `track_r_values_bitwise_eq` (bit-exact
float equality for NaN propagation), `track_r_assert_rows_eq`, and
`track_r_serialized_value_layout` (returns the expected
`(serial_type, payload_len)` pair for a given `SqliteValue`, used
by header-layout assertions).

New imports pulled in for the test module:
`parse_record`, `parse_record_into`, `serialize_record_iter_into`
from `fsqlite_types::record`; `serial_type_for_*` and varint
helpers from `fsqlite_types::serial_type`; `SmallText` from
`fsqlite_types`; `MAX_COLUMN` from `fsqlite_types::limits`;
`proptest::prelude::*` + `TestRunner` + `TestCaseError` +
`Config as ProptestConfig`; and `rusqlite::params_from_iter` /
`rusqlite::types::Value as RusqliteValue` for the oracle.
